subject: Sociology
title: Power and Authority
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Power and Authority.

hook.text: A robber and a tax collector both take your money. One has authority, the other has force. The difference matters.

buildup.text: Power is the ability to impose your will. Authority is power that people accept as legitimate.

discovery.text: Weber named three types: traditional (kings), charismatic (revolutionaries), and rational-legal (officials).

twist.text: Charismatic authority is unstable. When the leader dies, the movement must routinize or collapse.

climax.text: Modern states run on rational-legal authority. You obey the office, not the person. That's what makes transitions work.

punchline.text: Authority isn't about force. It's about why people obey.

quiz.question: What are Weber's three types of authority?
